title: Stream option for pulling images

comments.body:
@msisj tl;dr I think a adding the keyword `stream=True`  to the Post() call and using `response.iter_lines()` _should_ work. The compatibility call does not provide a method to provide progress output to caller. It returns either an Image or List[Image]. 

The go code for the podman API is a bit dense as we are supporting the compatible and libpod APIs. The RESTful endpoints are defined in `https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/pkg/api/server/register_*.go`, `r.Handle()` binds the path and method(s) to a go function, for example: `r.Handle(VersionedPath("/libpod/images/pull"), s.APIHandler(libpod.ImagesPull)).Methods(http.MethodPost)`

The image pull [API source]( https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/api/handlers/libpod/images_pull.go#L26) shows the service is always streaming the output as a concatenation of JSON objects, one per line. The service encodes and writes the JSON object as the libpod generates the output.

`api.stream_frames` is not needed in this case as it supports a multiplexed stream (stdout and stderr) from the service which is not the output from `pull()`.
